attacking_style: Prefers opportunistic moves from breakaway positions rather than direct GC attacks. Uses final climbs to bridge groups and fragment cooperation, typically attacking when already in advantageous break positions.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support, relying on tactical cunning and refusing work to conserve energy. Feigns weakness to avoid pulls, then commits when tactically optimal.
terrain_preference: Technical descents are a notable strength (gained time on Froome). Thrives in breakaway scenarios on mixed terrain. Severely exposed on high-altitude summit finishes.
vulnerability: Catastrophic weakness on high-altitude summit finishes (lost 4min, dropped from top-10). Marginal GC gains unsustainable against pure climbers on successive mountain stages.
key_patterns: Refuses pulls in breakaways to demoralize chasers and conserve energy (2+ instances)
Targets GC advancement from non-threatening breakaway positions rather than peloton-based climbing (3+ instances)
Uses tactical gamesmanship: feigning cramps, sitting on, fragmenting cooperation (3+ instances)
Technical descending ability gains time on GC rivals (Froome example)
Late-career GC resilience shown by holding race lead at age 39
Altitude/sustained summit finishes expose catastrophic weakness (4min losses)
form_trajectory: neutral: Showed remarkable durability holding Vuelta lead at 39 (2019), but commentary doubted survival on summit finishes, consistent with historical altitude weakness pattern
